configs.name: security_token
configs.comments:
Security token (a.k.a. session token) of this backend.

This is used for temporary credentials issued by Tencent Cloud STS
(e.g. `GetFederationToken` / `AssumeRole`). When `security_token` is
provided, it will be used together with `secret_id` and `secret_key`
to sign requests, and the `x-cos-security-token` header will be
attached automatically by the signer.

If this field is not set, OpenDAL will also fall back to reading
the token from environment variables `TENCENTCLOUD_TOKEN`,
`TENCENTCLOUD_SECURITY_TOKEN` or `QCLOUD_SECRET_TOKEN` (unless
`disable_config_load` is enabled).

configs.name: root
configs.comments:
Root path of this backend.

All operations will happen under this root.
Default to `/` if not set.

configs.name: master_addr
configs.comments:
Master address(es) in `host:port` format.

For single master: `"10.0.0.1:9200"`
For HA (comma-separated): `"10.0.0.1:9200,10.0.0.2:9200,10.0.0.3:9200"`

When multiple addresses are provided, the client uses
`PollingMasterInquireClient` to discover the Primary Master automatically.

Resolution precedence at `build()` time (highest → lowest), following
`goosefs-sdk` `docs/CLIENT_CONFIGURATION.md` §1:
1. This field (when set on the builder / OpenDAL config map)
2. `GOOSEFS_MASTER_ADDR` environment variable
3. `goosefs.master.rpc.addresses` / `goosefs.master.hostname` in
`goosefs-site.properties`

`build()` fails with `ConfigInvalid` only when **none** of the above
supplies a master address.

configs.name: block_size
configs.comments: Block size in bytes for new files (default: 64 MiB).

configs.name: chunk_size
configs.comments: Chunk size in bytes for streaming RPCs (default: 1 MiB).

configs.name: write_type
configs.comments:
Default write type for new files.

Supported values: `"must_cache"`, `"cache_through"`, `"through"`, `"async_through"`.
Default: `"must_cache"`.

configs.name: auth_type
configs.comments:
Authentication type.

Supported values: `"nosasl"`, `"simple"`.
Default: `"simple"` — PLAIN SASL with username.
`"nosasl"` — skip authentication entirely.

configs.name: auth_username
configs.comments:
Authentication username.

Used in SIMPLE mode as the login identity.
Default: current OS user (`$USER` / `$USERNAME`).

    # Root path of this backend.

    # Master address(es) in `host:port` format.

    # For HA (comma-separated): `"10.0.0.1:9200,10.0.0.2:9200,10.0.0.3:9200"`
    #
    # When multiple addresses are provided, the client uses
    # `PollingMasterInquireClient` to discover the Primary Master automatically.
    #
    # Resolution precedence at `build()` time (highest → lowest), following
    # `goosefs-sdk` `docs/CLIENT_CONFIGURATION.md` §1:
    # 1. This field (when set on the builder / OpenDAL config map)
    # 2. `GOOSEFS_MASTER_ADDR` environment variable
    # 3. `goosefs.master.rpc.addresses` / `goosefs.master.hostname` in
    # `goosefs-site.properties`
    #
    # `build()` fails with `ConfigInvalid` only when **none** of the above

    # master_addr="...",

    # Block size in bytes for new files (default: 64 MiB).
    # block_size="1000",

    # Chunk size in bytes for streaming RPCs (default: 1 MiB).
    # chunk_size="1000",

    # Default write type for new files.

    # Default: `"must_cache"`.

    # auth_type="...",

This connection string is used to connect to the mysql service. There are url based formats.

The format of connect string resembles the url format of the mysql client.
The format is: `[scheme://][user[:[password]]@]host[:port][/schema][?attribute1=value1&attribute2=value2...`

- `mysql://user@localhost`
- `mysql://user:password@localhost`
- `mysql://user:password@localhost:3306`
- `mysql://user:password@localhost:3306/db`

For more information, please refer to <https://docs.rs/sqlx/latest/sqlx/mysql/struct.MySqlConnectOptions.html>.

- AWS S3: `https://s3.amazonaws.com` or `https://s3.{region}.amazonaws.com`
- Cloudflare R2: `https://<ACCOUNT_ID>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com`
- Aliyun OSS: `https://{region}.aliyuncs.com`
- Tencent COS: `https://cos.{region}.myqcloud.com`
- Minio: `http://127.0.0.1:9000`

If user inputs endpoint without scheme like "s3.amazonaws.com", we
will prepend "https://" before it.

- If endpoint is set, we will take user's input first.
- If not, we will try to load it from environment.
- If still not set, default to `https://s3.amazonaws.com`.

configs.name: region
configs.comments:
Region represent the signing region of this endpoint. This is required
if you are using the default AWS S3 endpoint.

If using a custom endpoint,
- If region is set, we will take user's input first.
- If not, we will try to load it from environment.

configs.name: access_key_id
configs.comments:
access_key_id of this backend.

- If access_key_id is set, we will take user's input first.
- If not, we will try to load it from environment.

configs.name: secret_access_key
configs.comments:
secret_access_key of this backend.

- If secret_access_key is set, we will take user's input first.
- If not, we will try to load it from environment.

configs.name: session_token
configs.comments:
session_token (aka, security token) of this backend.

This token will expire after sometime, it's recommended to set session_token
by hand.

configs.name: role_arn
configs.comments:
role_arn for this backend.

If `role_arn` is set, we will use already known config as source
credential to assume role with `role_arn`.

configs.name: external_id
configs.comments: external_id for this backend.

configs.name: role_session_name
configs.comments: role_session_name for this backend.

configs.name: assume_role_duration_seconds
configs.comments: assume_role_duration_seconds for this backend.

configs.name: assume_role_session_tags
configs.comments: assume_role_session_tags for this backend.

configs.name: disable_config_load
configs.comments:
Disable config load so that opendal will not load config from
environment.

For examples:

- envs like `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`
- files like `~/.aws/config`

configs.name: disable_ec2_metadata
configs.comments:
Disable load credential from ec2 metadata.

This option is used to disable the default behavior of opendal
to load credential from ec2 metadata, a.k.a., IMDSv2

configs.name: skip_signature
configs.comments: Skip signature will skip loading credentials and signing requests.

configs.name: allow_anonymous

deprecated.note: Please use `skip_signature` instead of `allow_anonymous`

configs.comments:
Allow anonymous will allow opendal to send request without signing
when credential is not loaded.

configs.name: server_side_encryption
configs.comments:
server_side_encryption for this backend.

Available values: `AES256`, `aws:kms`.

configs.name: server_side_encryption_aws_kms_key_id
configs.comments:
server_side_encryption_aws_kms_key_id for this backend

- If `server_side_encryption` set to `aws:kms`, and `server_side_encryption_aws_kms_key_id`
is not set, S3 will use aws managed kms key to encrypt data.
- If `server_side_encryption` set to `aws:kms`, and `server_side_encryption_aws_kms_key_id`
is a valid kms key id, S3 will use the provided kms key to encrypt data.
- If the `server_side_encryption_aws_kms_key_id` is invalid or not found, an error will be
returned.
- If `server_side_encryption` is not `aws:kms`, setting `server_side_encryption_aws_kms_key_id`
is a noop.

configs.name: server_side_encryption_customer_algorithm
configs.comments:
server_side_encryption_customer_algorithm for this backend.

Available values: `AES256`.

configs.name: server_side_encryption_customer_key
configs.comments:
server_side_encryption_customer_key for this backend.

Value: BASE64-encoded key that matches algorithm specified in
`server_side_encryption_customer_algorithm`.

configs.name: server_side_encryption_customer_key_md5
configs.comments:
Set server_side_encryption_customer_key_md5 for this backend.

Value: MD5 digest of key specified in `server_side_encryption_customer_key`.

configs.name: default_storage_class
configs.comments:
default storage_class for this backend.

Available values:
- `DEEP_ARCHIVE`
- `GLACIER`
- `GLACIER_IR`
- `INTELLIGENT_TIERING`
- `ONEZONE_IA`
- `EXPRESS_ONEZONE`
- `OUTPOSTS`
- `REDUCED_REDUNDANCY`
- `STANDARD`
- `STANDARD_IA`

S3 compatible services don't support all of them

configs.name: enable_virtual_host_style
configs.comments:
Enable virtual host style so that opendal will send API requests
in virtual host style instead of path style.

- By default, opendal will send API to `https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/bucket_name`
- Enabled, opendal will send API to `https://bucket_name.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com`

configs.name: batch_max_operations

deprecated.note: S3 delete batch capability is enabled by default. Use CapabilityOverrideLayer to override delete_max_size for specific endpoints.

configs.comments: Deprecated: S3 delete batch capability is enabled by default.

configs.name: delete_max_size

deprecated.note: S3 delete batch capability is enabled by default. Use CapabilityOverrideLayer to override delete_max_size for specific endpoints.

configs.comments: Deprecated: S3 delete batch capability is enabled by default.

configs.name: disable_stat_with_override

deprecated.note: S3 stat override capabilities are enabled by default. Use CapabilityOverrideLayer to override them for specific endpoints.

configs.comments: Deprecated: S3 stat override capabilities are enabled by default.

configs.name: checksum_algorithm
configs.comments:
Checksum Algorithm to use when sending checksums in HTTP headers.
This is necessary when writing to AWS S3 Buckets with Object Lock enabled for example.

Available options:
- "crc32c"
- "md5"

configs.name: disable_write_with_if_match

deprecated.note: S3 write with If-Match capability is enabled by default and this option is no longer needed.

configs.comments: Deprecated: S3 write with If-Match capability is enabled by default.

configs.name: enable_write_with_append

deprecated.note: S3 append capability is enabled by default and this option is no longer needed.

configs.comments: Deprecated: S3 append capability is enabled by default.

configs.name: disable_list_objects_v2
configs.comments:
OpenDAL uses List Objects V2 by default to list objects.
However, some legacy services do not yet support V2.
This option allows users to switch back to the older List Objects V1.

configs.name: enable_request_payer
configs.comments: Indicates whether the client agrees to pay for the requests made to the S3 bucket.

configs.name: default_acl
configs.comments:
Default ACL for new objects.
Note that some s3 services like minio do not support this option.

configs.name: connection_string
configs.comments:
Set the connection_string of the sqlite service.

This connection string is used to connect to the sqlite service.

The format of connect string resembles the url format of the sqlite client:

- `sqlite::memory:`
- `sqlite:data.db`
- `sqlite://data.db`

For more information, please visit <https://docs.rs/sqlx/latest/sqlx/sqlite/struct.SqliteConnectOptions.html>.

configs.name: table
configs.comments: Set the table name of the sqlite service to read/write.

configs.name: key_field
configs.comments:
Set the key field name of the sqlite service to read/write.

Default to `key` if not specified.

configs.name: value_field
configs.comments:
Set the value field name of the sqlite service to read/write.

Default to `value` if not specified.

configs.name: root
configs.comments:
set the working directory, all operations will be performed under it.

default: "/"

configs.name: disable_create_dir
configs.comments:
Disable automatic parent directory creation before write operations.

By default, OpenDAL creates parent directories using MKCOL before writing files.
This requires PROPFIND support to check directory existence.

Some WebDAV-compatible servers (e.g., bazel-remote) don't support PROPFIND
or don't require explicit directory creation. Enable this option to skip
the MKCOL calls and write files directly.

Default: false

configs.name: enable_user_metadata

deprecated.note: WebDAV user metadata capability is enabled by default. Use CapabilityOverrideLayer to override write_with_user_metadata for endpoints without PROPPATCH support.

configs.comments: Deprecated: WebDAV user metadata capability is enabled by default.

configs.name: user_metadata_prefix
configs.comments:
The XML namespace prefix for user metadata properties.

This prefix is used in PROPPATCH/PROPFIND XML requests.
Different servers may require different prefixes.

Default: "opendal"

configs.name: user_metadata_uri
configs.comments:
The XML namespace URI for user metadata properties.

This URI uniquely identifies the namespace for custom properties.
Different servers may require different namespace URIs.
For example, Nextcloud might work better with its own namespace.

Default: `https://opendal.apache.org/ns`

configs.name: enable_conditional_read
configs.comments:
Enable conditional read support.

When enabled (the default), OpenDAL forwards the RFC 7232 headers
`If-Match`, `If-None-Match`, `If-Modified-Since` and
`If-Unmodified-Since` to the server when callers provide them.

Some WebDAV-compatible servers (e.g., nginx-dav) don't return ETags
in PROPFIND or don't honor these headers on GET. Setting this to
`false` drops the four `read_with_if_*` capabilities, so calls like
`reader_with(path).if_match(...)` return `ErrorKind::Unsupported`
locally instead of being silently ignored by the server.

Default: true
